rationale: Both papers are in the same marketing-finance stream on how firm communications/news affect shareholder value via abnormal returns: the citing study models news sentiment effects on abnormal returns, while the cited study analyzes short- and long-run abnormal returns to product preannouncements. The citing note explicitly says it “extends prior marketing-finance work,” and the cited paper is a plausible foundational/direct comparison source for that literature rather than a generic mention. Given this substantive topical and methodological overlap, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
